ksa_text: Knowledge of AI-specific attack and threat taxonomies, including adversarial examples, model evasion, model extraction, model inversion, membership inference, data poisoning, and prompt injection.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA RM-K-005 (Risk Management/Knowledge) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.438, highest: EU AI Act at 0.438). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. Master data management: MDM platforms, golden records, entity resolution, data matching/merging, reference data management, and master data governance.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.438, and Risk Management (RM) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this Risk Management KSA for Master Data Manager?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Risk Management competencies do not appear as Master Data Manager requirements in job postings. They are allocated to specialist roles. Exclusion consistent with market patterns.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld by market evidence. Risk Management is not a Master Data Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.
